definitions.definition: Handshake message body selected according to the HandshakeType.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: May be HelloRequest, ClientHello, HelloVerifyRequest, ServerHello, Certificate, ServerKeyExchange, CertificateRequest, ServerHelloDone, CertificateVerify, ClientKeyExchange, or Finished depending on HandshakeType.

definitions.definition: Cipher suites field in the DTLS ClientHello message.
Client protocol version field in the DTLS ClientHello message.
Compression methods field in the DTLS ClientHello message.
Stateless cookie value added to the DTLS ClientHello message.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: 0..2^8-1; when sending the first ClientHello, the Cookie field is left empty (zero length).
definitions.evidence:
opaque cookie<0..2^8-1>;                             // New field
...
When sending the first ClientHello, the client does not have a cookie yet; in this case, the Cookie field is left empty (zero length).

definitions.definition: A counter value that is incremented on every cipher state change.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: Initially zero; incremented each time a ChangeCipherSpec message is sent
definitions.evidence: struct { ... uint16 epoch; // New field ... } DTLSPlaintext; epoch A counter value that is incremented on every cipher state change. The epoch number is initially zero and is incremented each time a ChangeCipherSpec message is sent.

definitions.definition: Identical to the fragment field of a TLS 1.2 record.
definitions.evidence: struct { ... opaque fragment[DTLSPlaintext.length]; } DTLSPlaintext; fragment Identical to the fragment field of a TLS 1.2 record.

definitions.definition: Handshake message fragment length field in the DTLS Handshake structure.
Handshake message fragment offset field in the DTLS Handshake structure.
Handshake message type value for HelloVerifyRequest.
definitions.evidence: The HelloVerifyRequest message type is hello_verify_request(3).

definitions.definition: Identical to the length field in a TLS 1.2 record.
definitions.evidence: struct { ... uint16 length; ... } DTLSPlaintext; length Identical to the length field in a TLS 1.2 record. As in TLS 1.2, the length should not exceed 2^14.

definitions.definition: Alert level field used when generating a bad_record_mac alert for an invalid MAC.
definitions.evidence: If a DTLS implementation chooses to generate an alert when it receives a message with an invalid MAC, it MUST generate a bad_record_mac alert with level fatal

definitions.definition: Handshake message sequence number field used to order DTLS handshake messages.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: First message each side transmits in each handshake has message_seq = 0; incremented by one for each new message; retransmissions use the same value.
definitions.evidence:
uint16 message_seq;                               // New field

The first message each side transmits in each handshake always has
   message_seq = 0.  Whenever each new message is generated, the
   message_seq value is incremented by one.

definitions.definition: Handshake message type field in the DTLS Handshake structure.
Random field in the DTLS ClientHello message.
DTLS record field used for MAC computation and replay protection; concatenated with epoch to form a 64-bit value for MAC computation.
definitions.evidence: the sequence number used to compute the MAC is the 64-bit value formed by concatenating the epoch and the sequence number in the order they appear on the wire; DTLS records contain a sequence number to provide replay protection.

definitions.definition: The sequence number for this record.
definitions.evidence: struct { ... uint48 sequence_number; // New field ... } DTLSPlaintext; sequence_number The sequence number for this record. Sequence numbers are maintained separately for each epoch, with each sequence_number initially being 0 for each epoch.

definitions.definition: Server protocol version field in the DTLS HelloVerifyRequest message.
definitions.initial_value_or_range: DTLS 1.2 server implementations SHOULD use DTLS version 1.0 in HelloVerifyRequest.

definitions.definition: Session identifier field in the DTLS ClientHello message.
Equivalent to the type field in a TLS 1.2 record.
definitions.evidence: struct { ContentType type; ... } DTLSPlaintext; type Equivalent to the type field in a TLS 1.2 record.

definitions.definition: The version of the protocol being employed.
definitions.evidence: struct { ... ProtocolVersion version; ... } DTLSPlaintext; version The version of the protocol being employed. This document describes DTLS version 1.2, which uses the version { 254, 253 }.
